nodes.title: [2023-04-19] Evolution through natural selection is the process by which species gradually change over time in response to changes in their environment
nodes.description: Evolution through natural selection is a gradual process where species change due to environmental pressures, leading to the prevalence of certain genetic traits.

nodes.title: [2023-04-19] Natural selection has four steps: Variation, Inheritance, Selection, Time
nodes.description: The process of natural selection includes: 1. Variation: Members of a species exhibit variation in traits. 2. Inheritance: Traits are passed down through genes. 3. Selection: Environmental factors favor certain traits. 4. Time: Frequency of advantageous traits increases over time.

nodes.title: [2023-04-19] Peppered moths in England changed color due to industrial pollution
nodes.description: The classic example of natural selection is the peppered moth in England, where light-colored moths became more visible to predators due to pollution, leading to an increase in the black moth population.

nodes.title: [2023-04-19] Charles Darwin observed different beak shapes in Galapagos finches based on food sources
nodes.description: Darwin noted that finches on different Galapagos islands had varying beak shapes suited to their food sources, leading to adaptations and the development of new species over time.

nodes.title: [2023-04-19] Bacteria can evolve rapidly leading to antibiotic resistance
nodes.description: Bacteria evolve quickly due to short generation times, resulting in antibiotic-resistant strains, which pose significant challenges in modern medicine due to the overuse of antibiotics creating selection pressure.

nodes.title: [2023-04-19] Natural selection does not always lead to positive changes in a species
nodes.description: Natural selection can favor traits that are not beneficial in the long run, as seen in antibiotic-resistant bacteria, which may lead to dangerous infections.

nodes.title: [2023-04-19] Natural selection is limited by genetic variation within a population
nodes.description: The effectiveness of natural selection is constrained by the genetic diversity present in a population, affecting its ability to adapt to environmental changes.

nodes.title: [2023-04-19] Natural selection is a passive process that favors traits promoting survival and reproduction
nodes.description: Natural selection operates without a specific goal, favoring traits that enhance survival and reproduction based on environmental conditions.

nodes.title: [2023-04-19] Natural selection is one of many mechanisms contributing to evolution
nodes.description: Natural selection works alongside other mechanisms like genetic drift, mutation, and gene flow to shape the genetic makeup of populations over time.
